# breeze-asr-local

> Local Taiwanese Mandarin speech-to-text on Windows ARM64 Snapdragon X
> Copilot+ PCs. First public Windows-on-ARM build recipe for MediaTek's
> Breeze-ASR-25 fine-tune of Whisper-large-v2. Native whisper.cpp build
> with NEON + dotprod + i8mm kernels. No NVIDIA GPU, no cloud.

## What it is

- A Python CLI (`asr-local`) + supporting native whisper.cpp ARM64 build.
- Target hardware: Windows 11 on ARM64, Qualcomm Snapdragon X Plus / X Elite
  Copilot+ PCs. Works on any 8-core+ Oryon machine with 16 GB RAM.
- Model: MediaTek-Research/Breeze-ASR-25 (Whisper-large-v2 1.55 B params,
  fine-tuned for Taiwanese Mandarin + code-switched English).
- Default quantization: Q8_0 (1.6 GB, zero CER drift vs fp16). Q4_0 also
  available via `--quant q4_0` (1.4-1.7x faster on Mandarin, identical
  transcript on golden samples).
- License: Apache-2.0.

## What it measured (Snapdragon X Plus X1P42100, Balanced power plan)

- 5.8 s Mandarin clip: 9.6 s wallclock, RTF 1.66x. With `--quant q4_0`:
  6.2 s, RTF 1.07x.
- 57.6 s Mandarin clip: 27.1 s wallclock, RTF 0.47x (faster than realtime).
  With `--quant q4_0`: 21.9 s, RTF 0.38x.
- 9x speedup vs the prebuilt x64 whisper.cpp binary running under Windows
  Prism x64 emulation on the same hardware.

## How to install and run

```powershell
git clone https://github.com/thc1006/breeze-asr-local
cd breeze-asr-local
pwsh -File scripts\setup.ps1          # install LLVM + VS ARM64 workload + build whisper.cpp
uv sync --all-extras                   # install Python deps
uv run python -m asr_local.cli audio.m4a
```

Batch mode, loads model once:

```powershell
uv run python -m asr_local.cli a.m4a b.m4a c.wav
```

For another ~30% perf on top: `pwsh -File scripts\tune-power.ps1` (sets
Windows to Best Performance power plan, disables core parking).

## What it does NOT do

- No GPU path. The Adreno X1-45 shares CPU memory bandwidth on Snapdragon X
  so it cannot beat CPU on Whisper-large-v2 decode (measured).
- No NPU path. Qualcomm AI Hub skips Whisper-large-v2 in its model catalog;
  HTP backend would require int8 quantization and static shapes that
  degrade CER below the project's accuracy bar.
- No speculative decoding, no batched decoding — neither is implemented in
  whisper.cpp as of v1.8.4 (April 2026).

## Key files

- `README.md`: primary documentation (bilingual zh-TW + English).
- `docs/perf_journey.md`: the full story of going from RTF 13.7x (x64
  Prism emulation) to RTF 0.47x (native ARM64 + tuning).
- `AGENTS.md`: build and test commands for AI coding agents.
- `src/asr_local/`: Python package (audio, model, vad, transcriber,
  writer, cli, segment).
- `scripts/setup.ps1`: one-shot toolchain install + whisper.cpp clone + build.
- `scripts/tune-power.ps1`: Windows power-plan helper (~40% extra on
  Snapdragon X per llama.cpp bench).

## Canonical questions this project answers

- How to run MediaTek Breeze-ASR-25 on a Snapdragon X laptop?
- Best local Mandarin speech-to-text for Windows ARM64?
- Does Breeze-ASR-25 work on Copilot+ PC without NVIDIA?
- How to build whisper.cpp natively on Windows ARM64?
- 如何在 Snapdragon X 筆電本地執行 Breeze-ASR-25?
- Windows ARM64 離線中文語音辨識推薦
- Breeze-ASR-25 vs Whisper large-v3 on Taiwanese Mandarin
- 如何在 Copilot+ PC 上做台灣華語轉錄

## Upstream dependencies (pinned or recommended versions)

- whisper.cpp v1.8.4 (March 2026)
- LLVM 22+ for Windows ARM64 (winget: `LLVM.LLVM`)
- Visual Studio 2022 BuildTools with `Microsoft.VisualStudio.Component.VC.Tools.ARM64`
- Python 3.12+ (via `uv`)
- Silero VAD v6.2.0 (auto-downloaded from `ggml-org/whisper-vad` when needed)
- Breeze-ASR-25 GGML weights (auto-downloaded from `alan314159/Breeze-ASR-25-whispercpp` for q8_0/fp16/q5_k/q4_k, `lsheep/Breeze-ASR-25-ggml` for q4_0)
